justification: All rubric criteria met: 5 services recommended (Customer Account explicitly kept as shared module, not a 6th service) with strong justification tied to team size; Location Tracking correctly identified as separate due to 5K writes/sec; Payment correctly separated for ACID/PCI compliance; Strangler Fig migration explicitly recommended with phased approach; trade_offs thoroughly covers operational overhead, distributed tracing complexity, and team skill requirements; risks includes distributed monolith risk, saga failure handling (distributed transaction problem), and operational overwhelm for 8-person team.

observations.text: Explicitly advises against 6 services for 8 engineers — Customer Account kept as shared module with clear rationale
Location Tracking isolation justified by 5K writes/sec with CQRS pattern and time-series DB recommendation
Payment separation justified by both ACID consistency and PCI compliance boundary
Strangler Fig migration with concrete 4-phase, 6-month timeline starting with Location Tracking (lowest coupling)
Saga failure handling risk explicitly called out with compensating transactions and timeout mitigation
Team structure addressed — pair assignments to services with rotation, avoiding bus factor of 1
Event ordering and idempotency risks identified with Kafka partitioning and deduplication mitigations
Context map with DDD relationship types (Customer-Supplier, Shared Kernel, Open Host Service) adds depth
Order and Courier are separate services rather than combined initially — rubric notes they could reasonably stay together given tight domain coupling, though the separation is well-justified

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: recommends 5 core services (within 3-5 range) with strong justification; Location Tracking correctly identified as separate due to 5,000 writes/sec time-series workload; Payment correctly separated for ACID/PCI requirements; team size constraint explicitly addressed (notes 5 services for 8 engineers, rejects 8+ as too high operational burden); Strangler Fig migration pattern recommended with detailed phased timeline; trade_offs includes operational overhead and distributed tracing complexity; risks includes distributed transaction problem (payment authorized but courier assignment fails) with compensating transactions. Customer Accounts noted as extractable last or kept in monolith, showing pragmatic restraint.

observations.text: Excellent phased Strangler Fig plan with concrete week ranges and logical extraction order (Location first for isolation/ROI, Payment later after team gains confidence)
Distributed transaction risk explicitly called out with compensating transaction mitigation and dead-letter queue for stuck orders
Trade-offs section thoroughly covers operational overhead, second DB technology burden, and alternatives considered for each decision
Correctly advises investing in observability BEFORE extracting first service — 4-6 week foundation period
CQRS pattern applied specifically to Location Tracking where it's warranted, not blanket-applied

Lists 5 services plus Customer Account Service plus API Gateway — effectively 7 components. While Customer Accounts is noted as 'could remain in monolith' and API Gateway isn't a business service, the component list could give an impression of over-decomposition

Order and Courier are kept as separate services rather than combined initially — the rubric notes they 'can reasonably stay together initially' due to tight coupling. MJ does extract Courier last but doesn't discuss the option of keeping them merged
Pitfalls section explicitly warns against building a distributed monolith and shared databases — shows awareness of common microservice anti-patterns

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 5 services recommended with thorough justification; Location Tracking and Payment correctly identified as primary separation candidates with specific technical reasons (5K writes/sec, PCI/consistency); team size constraint explicitly factored (Customer Accounts absorbed to avoid 6th service, with clear rationale about 8 engineers); Strangler Fig migration pattern recommended with phased 3-quarter plan; trade_offs includes operational overhead and distributed tracing complexity; risks includes distributed transaction problem between Order and Payment Services.

observations.text: Excellent justification for 5 vs 6 services, explicitly citing 8-person team constraint and rejecting one-service-per-capability
Location Tracking separation justified by divergent write throughput (5K/sec append-heavy vs CRUD), with CQRS-lite pattern
Payment Service isolation justified by strict consistency and deployment independence, with synchronous call trade-off well-analyzed
Strangler Fig with concrete 3-phase quarterly plan, starting with lowest-risk extraction (Location Service)
Distributed transaction risk explicitly called out with saga-based mitigation, idempotency keys, and reconciliation
Operational maturity gap identified as critical risk with practical mitigation (observability stack first, 4-week stabilization gate)
Over-decomposition creep risk identified with governance rule — demonstrates awareness of team size constraint beyond initial design
Context map with DDD relationship types (Customer-Supplier, Open Host Service, Published Language) adds architectural rigor
Pitfalls section is exceptionally practical — shared databases, synchronous chains >2 hops, premature platform team
